title: Grok Build Bridge configuration
markdownDescription:
Configuration for **Grok Build Bridge** — one YAML, one command, one deployed X agent.

See [docs/build-bridge.md — Writing a bridge.yaml](../../docs/build-bridge.md#writing-a-bridgeyaml).

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`x_search`** — let Grok query the X timeline (posts, replies, trends). Use for any agent that reacts to the platform it will run on.
**`web_search`** — general web search. Use for fact-check / current-events agents; skip for purely on-platform bots to keep latency down.
**`code_execution`** — sandboxed code-execution tool. Use when the agent does math, parses data, or generates charts. Costs extra tokens.
ToolName.markdownDescription:
xAI tool the generated agent is allowed to call. The bridge's safety layer denies any tool not listed here.

See [docs/build-bridge.md — Safety guarantees](../../docs/build-bridge.md#safety-guarantees).

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`grok-4.20-0309`** — default production model. Good general reasoning, cheapest of the two. Pick this for most bridges.
**`grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309`** — multi-agent variant. Better at multi-step planning but ~3× more expensive. Use for agents that orchestrate other tools.
ModelName.markdownDescription:
Grok model id.

The bridge pins an explicit enum so a typo (`grok-4`, `grok-4.20`) fails fast at validation time instead of mid-deploy.

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`low`** — minimal deliberation. Fast, cheap, fine for formatting or templating tasks.
**`medium`** — the default. Balanced cost / quality. Use when you are not sure.
**`high`** — extended deliberation. Use for code generation or non-trivial reasoning.
**`xhigh`** — maximum deliberation budget. Use sparingly — it blows through `max_tokens_per_run` quickly.
ReasoningEffort.markdownDescription: Reasoning budget dial, forwarded verbatim to the xAI API's `reasoning_effort` parameter.

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`1.0`** — current schema. Only value accepted by grok-build-bridge ≥ 0.1.
version.markdownDescription: Schema version. Pinned to an enum so a future `1.1` / `2.0` cannot be silently consumed by an older bridge binary.

name.markdownDescription:
Slug used for the build directory, the X handle suffix, and the deploy target name.

Restricted to `^[a-z0-9-]{3,64}$` because downstream systems (X handles, Vercel / Render project names, DNS) reject anything else.

description.markdownDescription:
One-line summary of the agent. Capped at **280 characters** so a release can be announced in a single X post without truncation.

Also used as the default content for the pre-deploy X-post safety audit — make it readable.
examples: Posts a daily summary of trending AI topics on X.
Replies to mentions with project release notes.

build.markdownDescription:
How the agent source code is produced.

See [docs/build-bridge.md — End-to-end flow](../../docs/build-bridge.md#end-to-end-flow).

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`grok`** — stream the code directly from Grok. Requires `grok_prompt` and a valid `XAI_API_KEY`.
**`local`** — skip generation. The bridge expects the entrypoint file to already exist under `generated/<name>/` or next to the YAML.
**`grok-build-cli`** — shell out to the `grok-build` CLI shipped in the ecosystem. Falls back to `grok` when the binary is absent.
source.markdownDescription: Where the generated code comes from. Chooses between streaming from Grok, pointing at a pre-existing file, or delegating to the ecosystem CLI.

grok_prompt.markdownDescription:
Prompt handed to Grok when `source` is `grok` or `grok-build-cli`. Ignored for `local`.

Treat it as a system prompt — be explicit about the file layout, language, and the one thing the agent should do.
examples: Build a Python X bot that posts a daily summary of top AI papers.
Generate a TypeScript agent that replies to @mentions with project release notes.

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`python`** — default. Entrypoint defaults to `main.py`. All static safety checks run.
**`typescript`** — entrypoint defaults to `index.ts`. Runtime safety checks are limited to secret patterns.
**`go`** — entrypoint defaults to `main.go`. Runtime safety checks are limited to secret patterns.
language.markdownDescription:
Target language for the generated agent.

Enum-gated so the builder's templates stay honest; new languages need an explicit schema bump.

entrypoint.markdownDescription:
Relative path that the runtime executes.

Default resolved per-language at parse time:
- `python` → `main.py`
- `typescript` → `index.ts`
- `go` → `main.go`

required_tools.markdownDescription:
Explicit allow-list of xAI tools the agent may invoke.

Anything not listed here is blocked by the safety layer — the bridge denies by default.

deploy.markdownDescription:
Where and how the built agent is shipped.

See [docs/build-bridge.md — Deploying to X](../../docs/build-bridge.md#deploying-to-x).

markdownEnumDescriptions: **`x`** — hand off to `grok_install.runtime.deploy_to_x`. Falls back to writing `generated/deploy_payload.json` when the ecosystem is not installed.
**`vercel`** — run `vercel --prod --yes` in the generated dir. Requires the `vercel` CLI on `PATH`.
**`render`** — write a `render.yaml` and print git-push instructions. Render deploys happen on the git-repo side.
**`local`** — print the run command for the generated entrypoint. Great for a `--dry-run` end-to-end test.

runtime.markdownDescription:
Runtime glue used to deploy.

Free-form string so ecosystem plugins can register new runtimes without a schema bump.

post_to_x.markdownDescription:
When **`true`**, the agent posts autonomously after deploy.

Defaults to `false` so a dry-run never reaches the public timeline.

safety_scan.markdownDescription:
Whether to run the safety pipeline before deploy.

Leave `true` unless you have a very good reason — the bridge's whole value prop is 'safely deployed'.

schedule.markdownDescription:
Optional cron-ish schedule string.

Dialects differ by host, so the bridge does not parse this — your chosen runtime (Render, Vercel Cron, etc.) does.

personality.markdownDescription:
System-prompt personality snippet.

Capped at **500 characters** to protect the prompt budget and keep the generated agent's voice focused.
examples: friendly and terse — never uses exclamation marks
academic but cheerful; cites sources when possible

safety.markdownDescription:
Runtime safety knobs.

Optional because the defaults (audit on, sensible token cap) cover most users.

audit_before_post.markdownDescription:
Run a Grok-in-the-loop audit before publishing any content.

**Defaults to `true`** — turning it off is an explicit, reviewable choice.

max_tokens_per_run.markdownDescription:
Ceiling on xAI tokens per single bridge run.

Bounded to `[1000, 200000]` so a runaway loop cannot burn the monthly budget. Raise it for heavy code-gen runs; lower it for tight CI budgets.

lucas_veto_enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable the **Lucas veto** gate.

The Orchestra project wires this up as a hard-stop on unsafe output. In Bridge it stays off by default to avoid a hard dependency on Orchestra.
